key_points: A quadrilateral is a four-sided polygon whose interior angles add up to 360°
A parallelogram is a quadrilateral in which both pairs of opposite sides are parallel
Opposite sides of a parallelogram are equal and parallel
Opposite angles of a parallelogram are equal while adjacent angles are supplementary
The diagonals of a parallelogram bisect each other
Each diagonal of a parallelogram divides it into two congruent triangles
A rectangle is a parallelogram with four right angles
A rhombus is a parallelogram with all sides equal
A square is both a rectangle and a rhombus
examples: Parallelogram example: ABCD where AB ∥ CD and AD ∥ BC
Rectangle example: A quadrilateral with all angles equal to 90°
Rhombus example: A quadrilateral with all sides equal but angles not necessarily 90°
Square example: A quadrilateral with all sides equal and all angles 90°
formulas: Sum of interior angles of quadrilateral = 360°
Opposite angles of a parallelogram are equal
Diagonals of parallelogram bisect each other
Diagonals of a rhombus are perpendicular bisectors
Diagonals of a rectangle are equal
exam_tips: Use parallelogram properties to prove equality of sides and angles
Check if diagonals bisect each other to identify a parallelogram
Remember that adjacent angles in parallelogram are supplementary
Use congruent triangles formed by diagonals to prove properties
Draw clear geometric diagrams before solving proofs
